key_insight: A hybrid UNet-Transformer can learn to map composite microstructure geometry directly to stress and strain field images, acting as a fast surrogate for FEM.
engineering_relevance: This enables rapid field prediction for composite design and optimization, reducing reliance on expensive finite element simulations in microstructure-driven workflows.
method: Input: composite geometry images with square, hexagonal, and triangular tessellations
Model: hybrid UNet encoder-decoder plus Transformer attention for field reconstruction
Outputs: ten stress/strain field types across multiple boundary-condition and high-resolution datasets
evidence_signal: The model achieved strong subdataset performance, including R2=0.9991 and SSIM=0.9936 on periodic tessellation cases, with MAE below 0.05 on six of eight evaluated subdatasets.
watch_item: Performance drops on irregular square-grid cases with sparse soft-phase inclusions, showing the model can struggle with sharp stress discontinuities.
paper_title: Hybrid Unet-Transformer Model for Generating Stress and Strain Fields from Composite Geometrics
